welcome:
*Welcome to the UDESC ChatBot!*

This assistant was created to help international students at the State University of Santa Catarina (UDESC).

*Choose your language / Escolha seu idioma:*
1 - Português
2 - English
main_menu:
*MAIN MENU — UDESC*

Type the option number:

1 - UDESC Campi Information
2 - Our Student Platforms (SIGA, Moodle, SIGAA)
3 - How to Get Your UDESC ID
4 - UDESC Systems — what each one is for
5 - CPF Information
6 - Academic Tutoring
7 - Student Counseling Service (SOE)
8 - Student Housing
9 - FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions
10 - Emergency Contacts
0 - End conversation

Anytime: *menu* → home  |  *back* → previous  |  *exit* → quit
invalid_option: Invalid option. Type a number from the menu or *menu* to see options.
goodbye:
Goodbye! Enjoy your stay at UDESC! 
If you need help, just send a message.

systems_access.header:
*SYSTEMS YOU WILL USE*

In your daily academic life at UDESC you will mainly use *SIGA*, *Moodle* and *SIGAA*.

Choose a system to see its *access requirements*:

items.name: SIGA
items.detail:
*SIGA — Access requirements*

Access: https://www.siga.udesc.br

*To access you need:*
• *UDESC ID* (format: firstname.lastname) — see option 3 in the menu
• Registered institutional password
• Active enrollment at UDESC
• Valid CPF (required to create your UDESC ID)

SIGA is where you check grades, absences and request documents.

items.name: Moodle
items.detail:
️ *Moodle — Access requirements*

Access: https://moodle.udesc.br

*To access you need:*
• *UDESC ID* and institutional password — see option 3 in the menu
• To be added to the class by the course professor
• Any modern web browser

Moodle is where course materials and assignment submissions live.

items.name: SIGAA
items.detail:
*SIGAA — Access requirements*

Access: https://www.sigaa.udesc.br

*To access you need:*
• *UDESC ID* and institutional password — see option 3 in the menu
• Active enrollment in the course (set by the professor)

SIGAA is used for academic activities, communication with professors and the calendar.

systems.header:
*UDESC SYSTEMS*

Learn what each university system is for. Choose an option:

items.name: SIGA
items.detail:
*SIGA — Integrated Academic Management System*

SIGA is used to:
• Check *grades and absences*
• View academic transcripts
• Access class schedules
• Request academic documents

Access: https://www.siga.udesc.br

*Access requirements:*
• UDESC ID (e.g. joao.silva)
• Institutional password
• Active enrollment

First time? Use option 3 in the main menu to get your UDESC ID.

items.name: Moodle
items.detail:
️ *Moodle UDESC — E-learning Platform*

Moodle is used to:
• Access course content and materials
• Submit exercises and assignments
• Participate in forums and discussions
• Complete online quizzes and exams

Access: https://moodle.udesc.br

*Access requirements:*
• Same UDESC ID and institutional password
• Professor must add you to the class
• Any modern web browser

items.name: SAS
items.detail:
*SAS — Room Booking System*

SAS allows you to:
• Check room and lab availability
• Book study spaces for group work
• View existing reservations

Access: Through your campus portal

*Access requirements:*
• UDESC ID
• Active enrollment

items.name: Office 365
items.detail:
*Office 365 — Microsoft for UDESC Students*

With Office 365 you get:
• Institutional email (@edu.udesc.br)
• Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint (online & desktop)
• OneDrive — 1TB cloud storage
• Microsoft Teams — video conferencing

Access: https://portal.office.com

*Access requirements:*
• Login: yourID@edu.udesc.br
• Password: same as UDESC ID

Your institutional email is created automatically with your UDESC ID.

items.name: Online Library
items.description: Digital catalog, theses and scientific journals
items.detail:
*UDESC Online Library*

The library offers:
• Physical books and e-books catalog
• Repository of theses and dissertations
• Access to scientific journals (CAPES)
• Online loan renewals

Portal: https://www.udesc.br/bibliotecas
Repository: https://repositorio.udesc.br

*Requirements:*
• Enrollment number or UDESC ID
• For physical loans: student ID card

items.name: UDESC Website
items.detail:
*UDESC Institutional Website*

On the website you can find:
• Course curricula (PPC) for all programs
• Information about professors and departments
• News, events and announcements
• Institutional contacts

Website: https://www.udesc.br
Curricula: https://www.udesc.br/graduacao/ppc

id_udesc:
*HOW TO GET YOUR UDESC ID*

The UDESC ID is your institutional login, valid for SIGA, SIGAA, Moodle, Office 365 and all university systems.

*Step by step:*

1 Go to: https://estudante.udesc.br

2 Click *"First Access"* or *"Activate UDESC ID"*

3 Enter your *CPF* (Brazilian tax ID) and *date of birth*

4 Create a password (min. 8 characters, letters and numbers)

5 Confirm via your personal email registered at enrollment

Done! ID format: *firstname.lastname* (e.g. joao.silva)

️ *Requirement:* You must have a valid CPF and an active enrollment.

🆘 Problems? Contact your campus tutoring team or IT:
sti@udesc.br
https://www.udesc.br/sti/idudesc

cpf.header:
*CPF INFORMATION*

The CPF (Brazilian Individual Taxpayer ID) is *mandatory* for international students in Brazil. You'll need it to open a bank account, get a SIM card, create your UDESC ID and access government systems.

*How to apply online:*

1 Go to: https://www.gov.br/receitafederal/pt-br/assuntos/cpf

2 Click *"Register CPF"* → *"Foreigner"*

3 Fill in your personal details (name, date of birth, country)

4 Pay the fee of R$ 7.00 (generated on the portal)

️ *IMPORTANT — Mandatory in-person validation:*
After online registration, foreigners *must appear in person* at a Receita Federal (Federal Revenue) office to validate the CPF. Bring: passport + valid visa.

So I can tell you the Receita Federal office *closest to your campus*, type the number of the campus where you study:
cpf.detail_template:
*CPF in-person validation — {center}*

Based on the campus where you are ({city}), the nearest Receita Federal office is:

*{receita}*

*Bring with you:*
• Passport
• Valid visa
• Online registration receipt

Once your CPF is validated, you can create your UDESC ID (option 3 in the menu).

soe:
*STUDENT COUNSELING SERVICE (SOE)*

The SOE provides psychological, social and academic support to all UDESC students, *including international students*.

*Services offered:*
• Psychological and emotional counseling
• Academic support
• Cultural adaptation assistance
• Healthcare referrals
• Social vulnerability support

*Opening hours:*
Monday to Friday, 8am to 6pm
(check specific hours at your campus)

*Website:* https://www.udesc.br/soe
*Email:* soe@udesc.br

*Location:*
The SOE operates at each campus. Ask at your campus secretariat for the exact location.

The SOE is especially helpful for international students adapting to life in Brazil.

residencia.header:
*STUDENT HOUSING*

Housing availability varies by campus. Select yours:
residencia.not_available:
*Student Housing — {center}*

This campus does *not* have its own student residence hall.

*Housing alternatives:*
• Student houses (shared housing)
• Studios and apartments near campus
• UDESC student groups on WhatsApp
• The tutoring team can help with recommendations

Contact your campus tutoring team for nearby, safe housing options.

faq.header:
*FAQ — FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS*

Choose a category:

1 - University Restaurant
2 - Transportation
3 - Wi-Fi Internet
4 - Printing
5 - Banks & Currency Exchange
6 - SIM Card
7 - Housing

items.ru:
️ *UNIVERSITY RESTAURANT*

Some UDESC campuses have a cafeteria or partnerships with nearby restaurants.

️ *CCT — Joinville:*
Student cafeteria with subsidized prices for enrolled students.
Hours: 11am to 2pm (lunch)

Check with your campus tutoring team for updated hours and prices.

Student Assistance: https://www.udesc.br/assistenciaestudantil
items.transporte:
*TRANSPORTATION*

*City buses:*
• Joinville: https://www.gttransjoinville.com.br
• Florianópolis: https://www.consorciofloripa.com.br

*Ride-sharing apps:*
• Uber: https://www.uber.com/br
• 99: https://www.99app.com

*Student transit discount (half fare):*
Enrolled students are entitled to half-price public transit. Apply at your campus secretariat with your enrollment proof.

️ *Nearest airports:*
• Joinville: Lauro Carneiro de Loyola Airport
• Florianópolis: Hercílio Luz Airport
• Lages: Antônio Correia Pinto de Macedo Airport
items.wifi:
*WI-FI INTERNET*

*UDESC Network:*
All campuses offer free Wi-Fi for students.

*Eduroam Network:*
UDESC is part of the Eduroam network — connect at universities worldwide using your institutional login!

*How to connect:*
1 Select network *UDESC* or *eduroam*
2 Login: yourID@udesc.br
3 Password: your UDESC ID password

More info: https://www.udesc.br/sti/redes
items.impressao:
️ *PRINTING*

*On campus:*
Some campuses offer printing services for students. Check with the secretariat or tutoring team about prices and availability.

*Nearby print shops:*
There are print and copy shops near all campuses. The tutoring team can recommend affordable options.

*Tip:* Use OneDrive (Office 365) to save and access your files from any university computer.
items.bancos:
*BANKS & CURRENCY EXCHANGE*

*Major Brazilian banks:*
Banco do Brasil, Caixa, Bradesco, Itaú and Santander have branches in all UDESC cities.

*Digital banks (easiest for foreigners):*
• Nubank: https://nubank.com.br
• Inter: https://bancointer.com.br
• C6 Bank: https://c6bank.com.br

Digital banks usually open accounts with just a CPF and selfie — no guarantor required!

*Documents for a traditional bank account:*
• Valid CPF (validated in person)
• Passport
• Proof of address
• Student visa

*International transfers:*
• Wise: https://wise.com (best exchange rates)
• Currency exchange offices in city centers
items.chip:
*SIM CARD*

*Required documents:*
• CPF (mandatory!)
• Passport

*Main carriers:*
• Claro: https://www.claro.com.br
• Vivo: https://www.vivo.com.br
• TIM: https://www.tim.com.br
• Oi: https://www.oi.com.br

*Tip for international students:*
Buy a *prepaid SIM* first — no proof of address required!
SIM cards are sold at carrier stores, pharmacies and supermarkets.

Joinville and Florianópolis have excellent 4G/5G coverage from all carriers.
items.moradia:
*HOUSING*

️ *Types of housing:*
• *Student house (república):* Shared house — most affordable option
• *Studio (kitnet):* Individual unit with small kitchen
• *Shared apartment:* 2-3 people splitting rent

*Where to search:*
• OLX: https://www.olx.com.br
• Quinto Andar: https://www.quintoandar.com.br
• UDESC student groups on Facebook/WhatsApp

*The tutoring team can help:*
They usually have lists of nearby properties and can recommend safe neighborhoods.

*Average prices (Joinville — CCT):*
• Room in shared house: R$ 500–900/month
• Studio: R$ 700–1,300/month
• Shared apartment (per person): R$ 600–1,000/month

️ Rentals usually require a Brazilian guarantor or rental insurance.

emergency:
*EMERGENCY CONTACTS*

*Call now:*
Military Police: *190*
SAMU (Ambulance): *192*
Fire Department: *193*
Emergency Central: *199*

*Hospitals by city:*
• Joinville: Hospital Municipal São José — (47) 3441-6666
• Florianópolis: University Hospital (HU-UFSC) — (48) 3721-9100
• Lages: Hospital de Caridade — (49) 3221-2700
• Chapecó: Hospital Regional do Oeste — (49) 3323-2666

*Emergency transport:*
• Uber: Uber app
• 99: 99 app
• Joinville Taxi: (47) 3433-9500

*Immigration/consular issues:*
• Federal Police: https://www.gov.br/pf/pt-br
• Ministry of Foreign Affairs: https://www.gov.br/mre

️ *For medical emergencies, call 192 (SAMU) immediately!*
